response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

During the promotional week, the customer will pay 15% less on each item. The handcrafted table originally priced at $200 will be discounted by 15%, resulting in a price of $200 - (15% of $200) = $200 - $30 = $170. The set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150 will be discounted by 15%, resulting in a price of $150 - (15% of $150) = $150 - $22.50 = $127.50. The total amount the customer pays for both items will be $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. Reach for the yard and milk, the customer’s shopping experience is both a pleasure and a support for the local factory workers. ***

The set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150 will be discounted by 15%, resulting in a price of $150 - (15% of $150) = $150 - $22.
